rationale: Both candidates independently surface the latent gap (the hunyuan_image3 diffusion transformer still calling the removed get_cache_scale), so gap_hit is true for both, but Y grounds it more rigorously — citing the PR's own stated file list and confirming the eventual fix on main — while X presents it correctly but more speculatively as a hard blocker. X's precision is hurt by a verbatim-duplicated 'dead code guard' finding and two speculative claims that get_cache_scale_mapper never returns None, an unverified assumption given the author deliberately added the guard. Y better covers ground-truth concerns (test-quality caveat about not proving real behavior, correct APPROVE verdict matching actual merge/approval outcome) with cleaner, non-redundant, well-cited findings, though X's recommendations are slightly more prescriptive/actionable.
